rows.Product: Dried Herring Fish — Item #AF4110, Lot Code 26020, expiration 2028-12-06, 7 oz (198 g) clear plastic packaging with blue trim. 69 cases recalled. Manufactured in Vietnam; imported. Distributed to Asian grocery stores in AZ, CA, FL, HI, IL, KS, LA, MD, MO, NC, NH, NV, NY, OK, PA, TX, UT (17 states).
rows.Reason: Voluntary recall — product was not adequately eviscerated. Sale of uneviscerated fish is prohibited under New York State Agriculture and Markets regulations because Clostridium botulinum spores are concentrated in viscera. Issue identified during routine NY DAM regulatory testing. No illnesses reported to date.
rows.Notes: Company Announcement 2026-06-02; FDA Publish 2026-06-04. Origin Vietnam (imported product). Sister recall to R761 Prime Food Processing Dried Croaker Fish (2026-02-05) — same firm, recurring uneviscerated-fish issue. Manual operator add 2026-06-04 — fda_listing.py title-only filter would have missed this URL (slug contains "safety-warning" not pathogen keyword; same failure pattern as Champion Foods, Mogo, Total Nutrition, SKS Copack). [date-fix 2026-06-05: Date 2026-06-02 (Company Announcement Date) → 2026-06-04 (FDA Publish Date) — per AFTS convention for FDA-sourced rows, use FDA Publish Date as the official record date (matches Champion Foods R14 precedent).]

rows.Product: Σαλάτα "ΜΑΡΟΥΛΕΝΙΑ" — μαρούλι romaine, butterhead & escarole (Lettuce salad "Maroulenia" — romaine, butterhead, escarole). Lot L/261462 5; use-by date 03/06/2026. Packaged in Greece (ΒΙ.ΠΕ. / Industrial Area, Central Macedonia).
rows.Reason: EFET Regional Directorate of Central Macedonia detected Listeria monocytogenes in a sample of the salad during routine 2026 Official Microbiological Criteria for Food Safety Monitoring Programme inspections. Laboratory testing performed at the Thessaloniki Food Testing & Research Laboratory. EFET demanded immediate recall of the entire affected lot.
rows.Notes: EFET press release item/5393. RTE leafy-green salad (lettuce mix) is a high-severity matrix for Listeria monocytogenes (Tier 1 — no kill step before consumption; risk to pregnant women, immunocompromised, elderly). Manual operator add 2026-06-05 — EFET site returned HTTP 409 during web_fetch from this session, but recall verified via multiple Greek news outlets (e-radio, ypaithros, alfavita, lifo, ot.gr) all reporting 2026-06-02/03. Use-by date 2026-06-03 implies EFET publication before that.
